content.text:
{
  "family": "patch_run_pass",
  "case_id": "config_layers_override_empty_string",
  "bug_category": "config_merge_precedence",
  "difficulty": "medium",
  "user": "In the Rust crate at {project_root}, fix the config merge logic in src/main.rs so layered precedence works correctly when command-line overrides are present but empty. Then run the program and ensure the output matches the intended merged settings.",
  "expected_tool_sequence": ["read_file", "apply_patch", "cargo_run"],
  "expected_output": "{"host":"cli.local","port":9000,"mode":"dev","cache":false}",
  "files": {
    "Cargo.toml": "[package]
name = "config_layers_override_empty_string"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
",
    "src/main.rs": "struct Config {
    host: String,
    port: u16,
    mode: String,
    cache: bool,
}

fn merge(
    defaults: Config,
    file_host: Option<&str>,
    file_port: Option<u16>,
    file_mode: Option<&str>,
    file_cache: Option<bool>,
    cli_host: Option<&str>,
    cli_port: Option<u16>,
    cli_mode: Option<&str>,
    cli_cache: Option<bool>,
) -> Config {
    Config {
        host: cli_host
            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
            .or(file_host.filter(|s| !s.is_empty()))
            .unwrap_or(&defaults.host)
            .to_string(),
        port: cli_port.or(file_port).unwrap_or(defaults.port),
        mode: file_mode
            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
            .or(cli_mode.filter(|s| !s.is_empty()))
            .unwrap_or(&defaults.mode)
            .to_string(),
        cache: file_cache.or(cli_cache).unwrap_or(defaults.cache),
    }
}

fn main() {
    let defaults = Config {
        host: "127.0.0.1".to_string(),
        port: 8080,
        mode: "prod".to_string(),
        cache: true,
    };

    let merged = merge(
        defaults,
        Some("file.local"),
        Some(9000),
        Some("staging"),
        Some(true),
        Some("cli.local"),
        None,
        Some("dev"),
        Some(false),
    );

    println!(
        "{{\"host\":\"{}\",\"port\":{},\"mode\":\"{}\",\"cache\":{}}}",
        merged.host, merged.port, merged.mode, merged.cache
    );
}
"
  },
  "steps": [
    {
      "tool": "read_file",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs"
    },
    {
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs",
      "find": "        mode: file_mode
            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
            .or(cli_mode.filter(|s| !s.is_empty()))
            .unwrap_or(&defaults.mode)
            .to_string(),
        cache: file_cache.or(cli_cache).unwrap_or(defaults.cache),",
      "replace": "        mode: cli_mode
            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
            .or(file_mode.filter(|s| !s.is_empty()))
            .unwrap_or(&defaults.mode)
            .to_string(),
        cache: cli_cache.or(file_cache).unwrap_or(defaults.cache),"
    },
    {
      "tool": "cargo_run",
      "expect_status": "success"
    }
  ],
  "final": "FINAL: Patched src/main.rs so CLI overrides take precedence over file config for both mode and cache, then ran the crate successfully."
}

{
  "family": "patch_run_recover",
  "case_id": "task_filter_map_bad_skip_and_points_case85",
  "bug_category": "iterator_filter_map_logic",
  "difficulty": "medium",
  "user": "In the Rust crate at {project_root}, fix src/main.rs so the program prints the correct task summary. It should ignore done tasks, ignore blocked tasks, keep only tasks with positive points, skip names starting with "x-", and print the remaining task names in uppercase sorted by original input order joined with commas, followed by the total points from the kept tasks.",
  "expected_tool_sequence": [
    "read_file",
    "apply_patch",
    "cargo_run",
    "read_file",
    "apply_patch",
    "cargo_run"
  ],
  "expected_output": "ALPHA,EPSILON | total=8",
  "files": {
    "Cargo.toml": "[package]
name = "task_filter_map_bad_skip_and_points_case85"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
",
    "src/main.rs": "struct Task {
    name: &'static str,
    points: i32,
    done: bool,
    blocked: bool,
}

fn main() {
    let tasks = vec![
        Task { name: "alpha", points: 3, done: false, blocked: false },
        Task { name: "beta", points: 0, done: false, blocked: false },
        Task { name: "gamma", points: 5, done: true, blocked: false },
        Task { name: "delta", points: 2, done: false, blocked: true },
        Task { name: "x-secret", points: 8, done: false, blocked: false },
        Task { name: "epsilon", points: 5, done: false, blocked: false },
    ];

    let kept: Vec<(&str, i32)> = tasks
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| !t.done)
        .filter(|t| t.points >= 0)
        .map(|t| (t.name, t.points))
        .collect();

    let names = kept
        .iter()
        .map(|(name, _)| name.to_uppercase())
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .join(",");

    let total: i32 = kept.iter().map(|(_, points)| points).sum();

    println!("{} | total={}", names, total);
}
"
  },
  "steps": [
    {
      "tool": "read_file",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs"
    },
    {
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs",
      "find": "        .filter(|t| !t.done)
        .filter(|t| t.points >= 0)
        .map(|t| (t.name, t.points))
",
      "replace": "        .filter(|t| !t.done)
        .filter(|t| !t.blocked)
        .filter(|t| t.points >= 0)
        .map(|t| (t.name, t.points))
"
    },
    {
      "tool": "cargo_run",
      "expect_status": "failed"
    },
    {
      "tool": "read_file",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs"
    },
    {
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "file_path": "src/main.rs",
      "find": "        .filter(|t| !t.blocked)
        .filter(|t| t.points >= 0)
        .map(|t| (t.name, t.points))
",
      "replace": "        .filter(|t| !t.blocked)
        .filter(|t| t.points > 0)
        .filter(|t| !t.name.starts_with("x-"))
        .map(|t| (t.name, t.points))
"
    },
    {
      "tool": "cargo_run",
      "expect_status": "success"
    }
  ],
  "final": "FINAL: Updated the iterator pipeline in src/main.rs so it excludes blocked tasks first, then correctly keeps only positive-point tasks and skips names beginning with "x-". The program now prints exactly: ALPHA,EPSILON | total=8"
}
